description: Paired Shiab path rerun and comparison after all Phase XI changes. Runs both standard (identity-shiab) and paired (first-order-curvature) paths on fresh post-Phase-XI artifacts, plus a third metric-scaled-shiab operator for family scope.
bindingConstraint: D-P11-009: Neither standard nor paired operator is the canonically selected draft Shiab. No conclusion is inferred from stale pre-Phase-XI artifacts.

pairedPath.branchRobustnessResult: SAME as standard path (both implement S=F; same residual Upsilon=F-T)
pairedPath.conclusion: The paired first-order-curvature path produces identical numerical output to the standard identity-shiab path on the same inputs. Both implement S=F (identity on curvature). The paired path does not change any branch robustness or convergence conclusion.

thirdOperator.note: S=2*F is distinguishable from S=F on toy geometry (dimX=2). However, scalar rescaling does not change branch robustness classification — the residual Upsilon=2F-T=0 solution is a smooth deformation that preserves gauge-violation and solver-iteration fragility. Genuine Shiab family expansion requires dimX >= 4.

refinementConvergence.standardResult: bounded with non-monotone behavior on some quantities

repContentFatal.pairedResult: same: fatal is on the candidate (fermion-registry-phase4-toy-v1-0000), not on the Shiab branch
repContentFatal.conclusion: No change. The representation-content fatal is independent of Shiab operator choice.

overallConclusion: The paired first-order-curvature Shiab path has been rerun on fresh Phase XI artifacts. It produces identity-equivalent results to the standard path in all evidence categories. Adding the metric-scaled-shiab (lambda=2) third operator demonstrates numerical distinguishability but does not change any branch robustness or convergence conclusion. No stale pre-Phase-XI artifacts were used. The Shiab family remains open per D-P11-009.

provenance.notes: P11-M6 mandatory rerun of paired Shiab path after all Phase XI changes completed.
